# CLARITY-7 Trial Results: Nexoril for Chronic Fatigue Syndrome

## Study Overview

The CLARITY-7 (Clinical Assessment of Restorative Intervention Through Yielding-7) trial was a Phase III, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled, multicenter clinical trial evaluating the efficacy and safety of Nexoril (compound designation NXR-4821) for the treatment of moderate-to-severe chronic fatigue syndrome (CFS), also known as myalgic encephalomyelitis/chronic fatigue syndrome (ME/CFS). The trial was sponsored by Aurelius Pharmaceuticals and conducted across 43 clinical sites in the United States, United Kingdom, Germany, and Australia.

## Study Design

- **Phase:** III
- **Registration:** ClinicalTrials.gov identifier NCT05892341
- **Enrollment Period:** September 2023 to June 2024
- **Treatment Duration:** 24 weeks
- **Follow-up Period:** 12 weeks post-treatment
- **Completion Date:** March 2025

### Participants

A total of **1,247 participants** were randomized in a 1:1 ratio:

- **Treatment arm (Nexoril 150mg twice daily):** 624 participants
- **Placebo arm:** 623 participants

**Inclusion criteria:** Adults aged 18-65, diagnosed with ME/CFS per the 2015 IOM diagnostic criteria, Chalder Fatigue Scale score of 29 or higher at screening, symptom duration of at least 12 months.

**Exclusion criteria:** Concurrent autoimmune disease requiring immunosuppressive therapy, uncontrolled depression (PHQ-9 score > 20), pregnancy or planned pregnancy, prior use of investigational ME/CFS therapies within 6 months.

## Primary Endpoint Results

The primary endpoint was the proportion of participants achieving a clinically meaningful improvement, defined as a reduction of 7 or more points on the Chalder Fatigue Scale at Week 24.

| Outcome | Nexoril (n=624) | Placebo (n=623) | Difference |
|---|---|---|---|
| Responder rate | **62%** (387/624) | **34%** (212/623) | 28 percentage points |
| Mean CFS score change | -11.4 points | -5.8 points | -5.6 points |
| p-value | — | — | **p < 0.001** |

The treatment effect was statistically significant (p < 0.001) and clinically meaningful, with a number needed to treat (NNT) of 3.6.

## Secondary Endpoints

- **SF-36 Physical Functioning Domain:** Mean improvement of 14.2 points (Nexoril) vs. 6.1 points (placebo), p < 0.001
- **Pittsburgh Sleep Quality Index:** Mean improvement of 3.8 points (Nexoril) vs. 1.9 points (placebo), p < 0.001
- **Return to Work/Activity:** 41% of Nexoril participants reported returning to at least part-time work or structured activity, compared with 22% in the placebo group (p < 0.001)
- **Post-Exertional Malaise Frequency:** Reduced by 47% in the Nexoril group vs. 19% in placebo (p < 0.001)

## Safety Profile

Nexoril demonstrated an acceptable safety profile. The most common adverse events in the treatment arm were:

- Mild headache: 18.3% (vs. 12.1% placebo)
- Nausea: 11.7% (vs. 8.4% placebo)
- Dizziness: 7.2% (vs. 5.9% placebo)
- Upper respiratory infection: 6.8% (vs. 7.1% placebo)

Serious adverse events occurred in 2.4% of the Nexoril group and 2.1% of the placebo group, with no statistically significant difference (p = 0.72). No deaths were reported in either arm. Discontinuation due to adverse events was 4.2% (Nexoril) vs. 3.1% (placebo).

Hepatic transaminase elevations (>3x upper limit of normal) were observed in 1.1% of Nexoril participants, all of which resolved upon dose reduction or discontinuation. Quarterly hepatic monitoring is recommended.

## Mechanism of Action

Nexoril is a selective modulator of the neuroinflammatory JAK1/TYK2 signaling pathway. It targets microglial activation and neuroinflammation implicated in ME/CFS pathophysiology, reducing pro-inflammatory cytokine production (IL-6, TNF-alpha) while preserving adaptive immune function.

## Regulatory Status

Based on the CLARITY-7 results, Aurelius Pharmaceuticals submitted a New Drug Application (NDA) to the U.S. FDA in August 2025, with a PDUFA action date of April 2026. A parallel submission to the European Medicines Agency (EMA) was filed in September 2025. If approved, Nexoril would be the first FDA-approved pharmacological treatment specifically indicated for ME/CFS.
